If you're interested in supporting the program but unable to commit to taking an apprentice, donating software licenses, hardware, or training access makes a direct difference in what we can teach.

We use Apple devices, Sphero robotics kits, GitHub for source control, and Slack for communication. If your organization has surplus licenses, devices, or access to donate, we want to hear from you.

Reach out to Dave directly — he'll get back to you quickly and make the logistics easy.

The MCRI runs completely on generous donations. Every contribution — whether $50 or $50,000 — directly changes the trajectory of a student's life. You can fund a scholarship for a young woman's secondary education, or support the operating costs of the program itself.

In Zimbabwe, families pay for the equivalent of Junior and Senior year of high school. A donation of $600–$1,000 covers a young girl's school fees for an entire year — making the path to the MCRI possible.

MCRI students working together on a project

If you want to make the biggest impact on the lives of others, becoming an employer partner is the way to do it. As an employer partner, you commit to taking on an MCRI apprentice — and potentially hiring them full-time. This means coming to Victoria Falls, meeting students in person, and investing real trust in a young Zimbabwean professional.

Our employer partners tell us that within weeks, their MCRI apprentice is contributing meaningfully to their team. The quality of preparation is that high.

Download our Employment Partner Brief for the full details on how the partnership works, what we ask of you, and what you get in return.

Senior Director, Community Education Initiatives, Jamf

Dave founded and leads Jamf's Community Education Initiatives, which powers the MCRI and a global network of 28 MATTER Innovation Hubs across 8 countries. He built the employer partnerships that give MCRI graduates a direct path to full-time remote work, and he champions the program's commitment to gender equity and universal design. Dave is the person to contact if you want to get involved.

Kelly leads curriculum design and technical instruction at the MCRI. With deep expertise in Apple platforms and enterprise device management, she translates Jamf's real-world workforce requirements into a curriculum that produces graduates who are ready to work from day one. Kelly travels to Victoria Falls each cohort to deliver instruction in person alongside the MCRI team.

Valeria manages program operations, student support, and the logistics that keep the MCRI running cohort after cohort. She developed the TEAL+ instructional model that forms the foundation of every student's learning journey — before they ever walk through the MCRI doors. Valeria ensures every student has what they need to succeed, from curriculum resources to daily wellbeing.
